content.full_text:
I
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
January 26,2006
Brian Taugher
A Professional Corporation
980 9'h Street, Suite 1900
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-05-221
Dear Mr. Taugher:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Foundation
for Califomia Community Colleges regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act').r This adviie is based on the facts you have provided in
your request. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") does not act as a
finder of fact when it renders advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71; section
83114.)
QUESTION
Do the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions prohibit the Foundation for California
Community Colleges from hiring an employee of the California Community Colleges
Chancellor's Office?
CONCLUSION
No. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to individual ..public
officials' and do not apply to entities.
FACTS
The Foundation for Califomia Community Colleges (the "Foundation,,) is an
Education Code "auxiliary organization" (Education code $ 72670.5) and a putlic agency
belonging to PERS (Government code $$ 20056-51). hwas formed in 199i as a
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


I
J
File No. A-05-221
Page No. 2
non-profit public benefit corporation by the Board of Governors of the California
Community colleges. In200l, it was authoized.by the same Board to operate as a
system-wide auxiliary organization. It operates as an administrative arrn-of the
Chancellor's Office providing services to the community colleges throughout California.
Patrick Perry is an employee of the Chancellor's Office. The Foundation wishes
to hire him into a senior executive position. It is your understanding that as a public
agency and auxiliary organization it may do so even if he has worked on matters
involving the Foundation in the past. In short, your question is whether there is a
prohibition against the Foundations' hiring of Mr. Perry from the Chancellor,s Office.2
ANALYSIS
PorrNrrar. CoNrr,rct oF, INTEREsT
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will . perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported them." (Section g1001(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influenc" u gor"- ental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
Because the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to "public officials,,
who participate in a govelnmental decision that may have anlmpact orir" or more
economic interests held by that official, it would not be a conflicl of interest under the
Act for the Foundation to hire Mr. Perry. Whether or not a member of the Foundation
may have a conflict of interest in participating in the decision to hire would depend on
two factors:
(1) Is the Foundation a goveflrment agency; and
(2) If so, does any member participating in the decision have a disqualifuing
economic interest with respect to Mr. perry?
Although your question is posed as a 'tonflict of interest" question, there may be issues for Mr.
Perry to consider with respect to the Act's post employment restrictions. In a recent telephone
conversation, you indicated that you wish us to-proceed with addressing the conflict of interest question,
and that you will check with Mr. Perry to see if he wishes advice ."gu.iirg the other issues. In the
meantime, we are enclosing a copy of the Commission's Opinion, Iire Siigel, (1,977)3 FppC Ops. 62 and
two advice letters: the Francis Advice Letter, No. 4-86-214 and the Stone Adviceletter, No. 4-97-630,
which address concerns in determining whether or not an "auxiliary organization" of the type described
herein is considered a state agency with respect to certain provisions re'iating to the Act,s plst
governmental employment restrictions.
The Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifuing conflict of interest. (Regulation f aZOOlt;.y The general
rule, however, is that a conflict of interest exists whenever a public officiat makes a
govemmental decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one
or more of his or her financial interests.


t
File No. A-05-221
Page No. 3
t
You have not provided enough information for us to analyzethe first question.
with respect to the second question, you have not identified anyp".ro1who muy
participate in the decision or what economic interest that person may have that could
affect that determination. If you would like further advici on theses matters, please feel
free to submit another request.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By: w
Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
WJL:rd

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions prohibit the Foundation for California
Community Colleges from hiring an employee of the California Community Colleges
Chancellor's Office?

CONCLUSION: No. The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to individual ..public
officials' and do not apply to entities.
